what_it_does: Renders an interactive circular visualization of type nucleation: registry types as angular sectors, projected items as dots that fit inside the membership boundary, pile into candidate new-type blooms at the rim, or drift as residue along forbidden diagonals toward the box corners; includes a client-side birth-threshold dial, space/type/rung pickers, a minimal report, and a tappable hit layer.
observations: Uses React useState for the local 20/80 birth-threshold dial.
Uses framer-motion AnimatePresence and motion for animated points and candidate blooms.
Defines a local NucReport type and consumes Projection, NucParams, MotionFeel via props.
Computes layout from container size via useSize and centers the wheel at cx,cy with radius R.
Visually classifies points into three readings: inside (fits a type), pile (candidate new type), tail (residue).
Deterministic jitter via a sine-hash function avoids Math.random for stable re-renders.
Tail points are snapped to the nearest forbidden diagonal with depth driven by fit and a small deterministic spread.
Candidate blooms are placed at the mean angle of their cluster or fallback even spread.
Birth status is recomputed client-side from dial * pile_clustered without refetching.
Includes a transparent 15px hit layer so all points remain tappable.
Uses stamp() to attach semantic ui:// addresses to SVG elements and controls.
Picker is a local helper that renders a select only when options exist.
standards: The TYPE-NUCLEATION concept comment explicitly maps types to angular divisions and items to inside/pile/corner zones.
Three-way semantic placement: inside boundary, pile horizon, forbidden diagonal corners.
Client-side instant birth dial toggles born/forming state without server round-trip.
Deterministic tail placement using a pseudo-random sine hash keyed by point seq.
conventions: TypeScript React functional components with explicit props interfaces.
CSS custom properties (var(--...)) for colors and strokes.
Local helper functions and components defined at the bottom of the file.
Relies on imported utilities: placePolar, sectorHue, wedgePath, stamp, useSize, pointAddress, transition.
Adds aria-label and role to the SVG for accessibility.
concerns: zoneAngle is defined before zones but closes over it; works at runtime but ordering may confuse readers.
cornerPlace assumes theta and fit are meaningful for tail points, which is not verified here.
jitter01 is a simple sine hash and may show periodic clustering artifacts.
Hard-coded radii multipliers (1.18, 1.2, 1.55) could clip in very small containers.
Picker silently returns null when options are missing, with no empty-state feedback.
No visual handling for the unselected state beyond transparent stroke.
notes: A React wheel panel that turns a type-system projection into a three-zone radial map—fit, candidate new types, and residue—controlled by a local birth-threshold dial and registry pickers.
questions: What are the exact shapes of Projection, NucParams, and MotionFeel?
How does the engine compute pile_cluster, tail, inside, fit, theta, and r for each point?
What do avail.item_spaces, types_spaces, and rungs represent and how are they populated?
Is sectorHue deterministic and consistent with the sector index mapping used here?
Does stamp require special handling for ids containing characters like ✦?
Are the forbidden diagonals purely decorative or do they encode a meaningful seed/axis concept?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated TYPE-NUCLEATION purpose matches the implementation: it visualizes registry types as angular divisions, fits inside the boundary, misfits as piles/candidates, residue as corners, and provides a birth dial and pickers.
apparent_themes: Type-as-geometry: registry types divide the circle.
Read-only projection with local control parameters.
intention_signals: It tries to make the abstract process of deciding when a pile of misfits becomes a new registry type visually legible as a radial instrument, letting the user tune the birth threshold and choose which spaces/rungs to type against.
novelty: The combination of a 20/80 birth dial, candidate blooms placed at true cluster angles, and tail points explicitly pushed to box corners along forbidden diagonals to signal grow-the-box / new-axis is a distinctive visualization pattern.
contribution: Provides a concrete, self-contained React visualization for a type-nucleation model: radial sectors, membership boundary, pile horizon, residue diagonals, animated points, candidate blooms, a client-side birth-threshold dial, and registry-space pickers, all wired to a tappable hit layer and semantic stamps.
summary_for_embedding: Nucleation.tsx is a React component that renders an interactive radial wheel visualizing type nucleation: registry types as angular sectors, items as dots that fit inside, pile into candidate new-type blooms at the rim, or drift as residue toward box corners, with a client-side birth-threshold dial and space/rung pickers.
intention_for_embedding: To give users an immediate, spatial reading of which projected items fit existing registry types, which form candidate new types, and which remain untypeable residue, while letting them tune the threshold for declaring a new type and select the typed spaces/rungs.

symbols.does: Local type alias describing the nucleation report payload, including pile counts, zone metadata, and available spaces/rungs.
symbols.description: NucReport defines the shape of the projection.nucleation data consumed by the component: counts of clustered, tail, born, distinct, and total piles; a list of zones with ids, exemplars, born/distinct flags, and sizes; plus available item spaces, type spaces, and rungs for the pickers.

symbols.does: Exported React component that renders the full nucleation wheel, report bar, birth dial, and registry pickers.
symbols.description: Nucleation receives a Projection, motion feel, selected sequence, pick callback, and NucParams state. It measures its container, draws type sectors, membership and pile-horizon circles, forbidden diagonal lines, animated data points, candidate new-type blooms, a hit layer, a three-line report, and controls for the birth threshold and typed spaces/rungs.

symbols.does: Maps a candidate zone index to the mean angular position of its cluster, falling back to an even spread.
symbols.description: zoneAngle looks up the average theta of points assigned to the given pile_cluster and returns that angle minus π/2 for SVG placement. If no cluster data exists, it distributes the zone evenly around the circle based on zones.length.

symbols.does: Deterministic pseudo-random number in [0,1) derived from a sine hash of an integer seed.
symbols.description: jitter01 avoids Math.random so that tail and corner placements remain stable across renders. It uses a classic sin/fract hash pattern and is used by cornerPlace to add small radial and angular spread around a diagonal corner.

symbols.does: Places a tail/residue point near the nearest forbidden box diagonal, deeper for worse fit and slightly jittered.
symbols.description: cornerPlace snaps the given theta to the closest diagonal angle, then computes a radius between roughly 1.26R and 1.58R based on fit and deterministic jitter, plus a small angular spread. This visually separates untypeable residue from candidate piles and pushes it toward the box corner.

symbols.does: Local helper component that renders a labeled select control when options are provided.
symbols.description: Picker is a small form control used for the items, types, and rung pickers. It receives a name, label, current value, option list, and callback; it renders null if no options exist, otherwise a label wrapping a native select.
